words.name: Apple
words.description:
A round fruit with firm, white flesh and skin.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'X' handshape (index finger bent).
2. Place the knuckle of the index finger on the cheek.
3. Twist the hand once or twice.
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'X' handshape (index finger bent). 2. Place the knuckle of the index finger on the cheek. 3. Twist the hand once or twice.

words.name: Banana
words.description:
A long, curved fruit with a yellow peel.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant '1' handshape.
2. Pull the index finger down on the non-dominant index finger (simulating peeling a banana).
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant '1' handshape. 2. Pull the index finger down on the non-dominant index finger (simulating peeling a banana).

words.name: Orange
words.description:
A round, juicy citrus fruit.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'C' handshape.
2. Squeeze the hand twice in front of the mouth.
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'C' handshape. 2. Squeeze the hand twice in front of the mouth.

words.name: Grapes
words.description:
A small, round, juicy fruit.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant '5' handshape, fingers wiggling.
2. Start at the elbow and move the hand down the non-dominant arm (simulating a cluster).
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant '5' handshape, fingers wiggling. 2. Start at the elbow and move the hand down the non-dominant arm (simulating a cluster).

words.name: Strawberry
words.description:
A red, juicy fruit with seeds on the surface.
How to sign:
1. Place the dominant 'R' shape on the chin.
2. Slide the finger downward twice.
3. Then use the non-dominant handshape 'I' and touch it by all of the fingers to simulate a fruit.
words.howToSign:
1. Place the dominant 'R' shape on the chin. 
2. Slide the finger downward twice.
3. Then use the non-dominant handshape 'I' and touch it by all of the fingers to simulate a fruit.

words.name: Watermelon
words.description:
A large, round fruit with a hard green rind and sweet, red, pulpy flesh.
How to sign:
1. Close your non-dominant hand and flick it by the dominant hand.
words.howToSign: 1. Close your non-dominant hand and flick it by the dominant hand.

words.name: Mango
words.description:
A fleshy, oval, yellowish-red tropical fruit.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant 'M' handshape to scoop the flat palm of the non-dominant hand.
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant 'M' handshape to scoop the flat palm of the non-dominant hand.

words.name: Pineapple
words.description:
A large, juicy tropical fruit with spiky leaves.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant 'P' handshape and move it up the face in an arc.
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant 'P' handshape and move it up the face in an arc.

words.name: Peach
words.description:
A round fruit with fuzzy skin and a large stone.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant 'P' handshape and touch it to the cheeks.
2. Twist the 'P' handshape back and forth.
words.howToSign:
1. Use the dominant 'P' handshape and touch it to the cheeks.
2. Twist the 'P' handshape back and forth.

words.name: Cherry
words.description:
A small, round, red fruit with a stone.
How to sign:
1. Sign the 'Red Color' sign by placing the tip of the index finger on the chin.
2. Slide the finger downward twice.
3. Then use the non-dominant handshape 'I' and touch it by all of the fingers to simulate a fruit in a stem.
words.howToSign:
1. Sign the 'Red Color' sign by placing the tip of the index finger on the chin. 
2. Slide the finger downward twice.
3. Then use the non-dominant handshape 'I' and touch it by all of the fingers to simulate a fruit in a stem.

words.name: Fruits
words.description: The edible reproductive body of a seed plant, typically sweet and fleshy.
